steps.instructions: Inspect the scan room for equipment that should not be present during scanning, such as wheelchairs, pumps, monitors, cables, tools, carts, or loose accessories. Confirm all items in the room are MRI safe or MRI conditional and appropriate for the scan environment. Expected outcome: No unauthorized or questionable equipment is contributing to RF noise, distortion, or safety risk.

steps.title: Check for External RF Noise Sources
steps.instructions: Confirm the scan room door is fully closed and latching properly during the scan. Look for items that may interfere with RF shielding, including cables passing through the door, damaged door seals, loose panels, or open penetration panel covers. Expected outcome: The MRI room shielding path is intact during scanning.

steps.title: Inspect Coil Selection and Positioning
steps.instructions: Verify the correct coil is selected for the exam or QA scan. Confirm the coil is positioned correctly, centered over the anatomy or phantom, and not tilted, twisted, or partially outside the expected imaging area. Expected outcome: The coil setup matches the protocol and scan region.

steps.title: Inspect Coil Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: With the system safe and not actively scanning a patient, inspect the coil cable routing, connector seating, and visible cable condition. Look for loose connections, bent connector pins, damaged cable jackets, excessive strain, or cables routed under the patient/table in a way that could affect contact or positioning. Expected outcome: Coil connections are secure and no obvious accessory damage is present.

steps.title: Swap to a Known-Good Coil When Appropriate
steps.instructions: If the artifact appears linked to a specific coil, repeat a basic test using another compatible known-good coil or coil element setup. If the artifact disappears with another coil, remove the suspect coil from service and document the finding. Expected outcome: A coil-related fault is confirmed or ruled out without internal system disassembly.

steps.title: Verify Phantom and QA Setup
steps.instructions: For failed QA scans, confirm the correct phantom is being used and is placed according to site procedure. Check phantom fill level if visible, positioning, centering, orientation, and that the correct QA protocol is selected. Expected outcome: QA failure is not caused by incorrect phantom placement, wrong protocol, or poor setup.

steps.title: Check Patient or Phantom Positioning
steps.instructions: Confirm the anatomy or phantom is properly centered in the magnet bore and positioned according to the selected protocol. Off-center positioning can contribute to shading, distortion, failed uniformity checks, or inconsistent QA results. Expected outcome: Positioning-related image quality problems are ruled out.

steps.title: Review Protocol and Sequence Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct protocol, coil configuration, scan plane, field of view, slice thickness, bandwidth, and acceleration settings were used. If the issue began after protocol changes, compare against a known-good saved protocol or standard site protocol. Expected outcome: The artifact is not caused by an incorrect or modified scan setup.

steps.title: Check for Patient-Related Artifact Sources
steps.instructions: If the artifact occurred during a patient exam, confirm whether motion, metal implants, monitoring leads, clothing, medication patches, or external devices could have contributed. Do not assume system failure until patient-specific causes are considered. Expected outcome: Patient-related causes are separated from equipment-related causes.

steps.title: Check Table and Accessory Positioning
steps.instructions: Confirm the table moves smoothly, locks into the expected scan position, and is not obstructed. Verify pads, cushions, straps, positioning aids, and accessories are not interfering with coil placement or causing poor patient positioning. Expected outcome: Table or accessory setup is not contributing to distortion, shading, or repeat QA failures.

steps.title: Review Recent Environmental or Facility Changes
steps.instructions: Ask whether construction, new equipment installation, electrical work, network cabling, room modifications, or shielding work occurred recently near the MRI suite. New external interference sources can cause RF noise or image artifacts even when the scanner itself has not changed. Expected outcome: Facility-related causes are identified for further investigation if needed.

steps.title: Power Cycle Only If Approved by Site Procedure
steps.instructions: If the system is not in patient use and site policy allows, perform an approved system restart or controlled power cycle. Do not perform emergency shutdowns or service-level resets unless trained and authorized. Expected outcome: Temporary software or acquisition communication issues may clear, or the artifact remains reproducible.

steps.title: Repeat a Controlled Test Scan
steps.instructions: After correcting any setup, coil, room, or protocol issue, repeat the appropriate QA or phantom scan. Compare the result to the original failure. Expected outcome: If the scan passes and the artifact is gone, return the system to service according to site policy and stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Remove Suspect Accessories from Service
steps.instructions: If a specific coil, cable, phantom, pad, or accessory is linked to the artifact or QA failure, remove that item from service. Label it clearly and route it for repair, replacement, or vendor evaluation. Expected outcome: The scanner is not blamed for an accessory-related fault, and the unsafe or unreliable accessory is controlled.

CCR.Complaint: MRI technologist reported RF noise and failed phantom QA scan on the Siemens MAGNETOM Vida.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found the scan room door was not fully latching during QA, allowing intermittent RF noise during acquisition.
CCR.Resolution: Door obstruction was removed, QA scan was repeated successfully, and the MAGNETOM Vida was returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the issue occurred during patient scan or QA
Whether all coils or one coil were affected
Scan room door fully closed and latched
Any new equipment or construction nearby
Whether the artifact was intermittent or repeatable
QA result before and after troubleshooting

title: Siemens Healthineers MAGNETOM Vida MRI System - Cooling, Helium, Compressor, Host, Or Reconstruction System Fault
description: Troubleshooting MRI system faults related to cooling, helium status, compressor operation, host startup, or image reconstruction availability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is inside the bore before beginning troubleshooting.

If a scan is in progress and the system reports a cooling, helium, compressor, host, or reconstruction fault, stop clinical use according to site procedure and move patient care to another available MRI system if needed.

Expected outcome: No patient remains dependent on a system with unresolved environmental, magnet, or computing faults.

steps.title: Check for Active Magnet or Cryogen Safety Alarms
steps.instructions:
Look for any active helium, magnet pressure, cold head, compressor, quench pipe, or magnet monitoring alarms.

Do not silence or ignore cryogen-related alarms without confirming the alarm condition.

Expected outcome: Any magnet safety concern is identified before routine troubleshooting continues.

If helium pressure, quench, oxygen depletion, or magnet emergency alarms are present, stop troubleshooting and escalate immediately.

steps.title: Verify Room Conditions
steps.instructions:
Check MRI equipment room and scanner room temperature, airflow, and humidity conditions.

Confirm the HVAC system is operating and that vents are not blocked by carts, linen, covers, or construction materials.

Expected outcome: Room conditions are within site requirements and not contributing to cooling or electronics faults.

steps.title: Check Facility Chilled Water or Cooling Supply
steps.instructions:
Verify whether the site uses facility chilled water, a dedicated chiller, or another cooling loop supporting the MRI equipment.

Check for facility alarms, closed valves, low flow indications, leaks, pump failures, or recent building maintenance.

Expected outcome: Cooling water or coolant flow is available and stable.

If a facility cooling issue is found, contact facilities and do not return the MRI to service until cooling is restored and system status is normal.

steps.title: Inspect the Compressor Area Externally
steps.instructions:
Check the helium compressor or cold head compressor area for power, abnormal noise, vibration, heat, odor, leaks, or tripped disconnects.

Do not open compressor covers or perform internal compressor repair unless trained and authorized.

Expected outcome: The compressor is powered, running normally, and not showing signs of overheating or failure.

steps.title: Verify Electrical Power to Support Equipment
steps.instructions:
Confirm power is available to the MRI electronics cabinets, host computer, reconstruction hardware, compressor, chiller support equipment, and related network equipment.

Check for tripped breakers only if allowed by site policy.

Expected outcome: All required support systems have normal power and no obvious facility power issue exists.

steps.title: Check for Recent Power Events or Planned Work
steps.instructions:
Ask MRI staff and facilities whether there was a power outage, UPS alarm, HVAC interruption, chiller maintenance, network work, software update, or scanner restart before the fault appeared.

Expected outcome: Recent external events are identified and documented.

If the fault followed a power or cooling interruption, allow only approved system recovery steps and escalate if the system does not return to normal.

steps.title: Review System Console Messages
steps.instructions:
At the MRI operator console, review the active messages related to cooling, helium, compressor, host, reconstruction, image processing, or system availability.

Note the exact wording, time, and whether the message blocks scanning or only limits image reconstruction.

Expected outcome: The fault is clearly identified and not treated as a vague “scanner down” issue.

steps.title: Confirm Host Computer Status
steps.instructions:
Check whether the host computer, operator console, monitor, keyboard, mouse, and system interface are responsive.

If the host is frozen, avoid repeated forced restarts unless approved by site procedure.

Expected outcome: The host is either responsive, recoverable through normal restart, or clearly failed and needs escalation.

steps.title: Check Reconstruction System Availability
steps.instructions:
Confirm whether raw data acquisition, image reconstruction, prior image review, and sending images to PACS are functioning.

A reconstruction fault may allow some system functions while preventing completed images from being generated or transferred.

Expected outcome: The failure is narrowed to reconstruction, host control, network transfer, or full scanner operation.

steps.title: Verify Network and PACS/DICOM Connectivity
steps.instructions:
If reconstruction completes but images do not appear where expected, verify scanner network connection, PACS destination status, DICOM queue behavior, and whether other imaging systems are affected.

Expected outcome: A network or PACS issue is separated from an MRI reconstruction hardware/software fault.

If the issue affects multiple imaging systems, escalate to IT/PACS rather than treating the Vida as the only failed device.

steps.title: Perform an Approved System Restart Only When Safe
steps.instructions:
If no magnet safety alarm is active, no cooling emergency exists, and the scanner is not in patient use, perform only the normal approved shutdown/restart process for the console or system.

Do not power-cycle MRI cabinets, compressor equipment, or reconstruction hardware by breaker unless directed by Siemens service or local policy.

Expected outcome: Temporary host or reconstruction software faults clear after a controlled restart.

If the fault clears, verify system readiness and document the action. Stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Run a Basic Operational Check
steps.instructions:
After recovery, confirm the system reaches normal ready state, console operation is stable, no active cooling or helium faults remain, reconstruction completes, and images can be reviewed or transferred as expected.

Expected outcome: The MRI is safe and functional for clinical use according to site return-to-service policy.

steps.title: Check for Recurring Faults
steps.instructions:
If the same cooling, helium, compressor, host, or reconstruction fault returns after restart or after a short period of operation, remove the system from clinical use.

Expected outcome: Intermittent or recurring faults are not dismissed as resolved.

CCR.Complaint: MRI staff reported the Siemens MAGNETOM Vida displayed a cooling/compressor fault and would not allow normal scanning.
CCR.Cause: External checks found facility chilled water flow was unavailable after building maintenance, causing the MRI cooling fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the MRI from service, notified facilities, verified cooling restoration, confirmed no active system alarms after recovery, and returned the unit to service after operational checks.

helpfulDetails: Whether patient was on table or scan was active
Room temperature and equipment room temperature
Compressor running, stopped, noisy, hot, or alarming
Any helium, magnet pressure, or quench-related alarms
Recent power, HVAC, network, or maintenance events
Final device status: returned to service or out of service

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS zee Series Angiography System - C-arm movement, brake, collision, or positioner fault
description: Troubleshooting ARTIS zee C-arm motion, brake, collision sensing, table clearance, or positioner faults using safe external checks first.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm whether a patient is on the table or inside the interventional workspace. If the C-arm, stand, table, or positioner movement is unreliable, stop motion immediately and notify clinical staff. Expected outcome: No patient, staff member, sterile field, catheter, tubing, or accessory is placed at risk from unexpected movement. If patient care is active, move to a safe clinical workflow before continuing. Do not troubleshoot motion faults over an active patient unless site procedure and clinical leadership confirm it is safe.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Movement or Positioner Symptom
steps.instructions: Ask the operator what failed and when it occurred. Identify whether the issue involves: C-arm will not move; Movement stops unexpectedly; Brake will not release; Brake will not hold; Collision warning or collision stop; Position not reached; Table-to-C-arm clearance issue; Positioner control not responding; Intermittent motion fault during angulation or rotation. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before resets, part swaps, or escalation.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Obstructions Around the System
steps.instructions: Inspect the C-arm travel path, floor area, table base, ceiling or floor stand path, detector area, tube side, monitor suspension, foot controls, carts, cables, sterile drapes, lead shields, step stools, and accessory rails. Remove any item that could contact the C-arm, detector, tube housing, table, or stand. Expected outcome: The system path is clear, and motion can resume if the fault was caused by a physical obstruction. If removing the obstruction resolves the issue, verify safe motion through a limited range, document the cause, and stop.

steps.title: Verify the Table and Patient Position Are Not Creating a Collision Condition
steps.instructions: Check table height, lateral position, longitudinal position, tabletop extension, patient support accessories, arm boards, head holders, anesthesia equipment, and draping. Reposition only with clinical approval and only when safe for the patient. Expected outcome: The ARTIS zee positioner has adequate clearance to move without triggering collision protection. If repositioning clears the collision warning and movement returns, document the obstruction or clearance issue and stop.

steps.title: Check the Active Collision or Safety Message
steps.instructions: Review the system display, room control, or operator console for the exact warning or fault message. Do not repeatedly override or force movement if the system reports collision, brake, or positioner protection. Expected outcome: The exact system-reported fault is captured for troubleshooting and service documentation.

steps.title: Confirm Emergency Stop Buttons Are Released
steps.instructions: Check all accessible emergency stop buttons in the room, including wall stations, table-side controls, system control panels, and operator locations. Make sure no emergency stop is latched or partially pressed. Expected outcome: Motion is not inhibited by an active emergency stop circuit. If releasing an emergency stop restores movement, verify why it was activated, perform a safe functional check, document the finding, and stop.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Controls, Joysticks, Footswitches, and Room Controls
steps.instructions: Check that C-arm controls are not stuck, damaged, unplugged, obstructed by drapes, or being pressed unintentionally. Verify that no control device is wedged against equipment or flooring. Expected outcome: The system is not receiving conflicting, stuck, or unsafe motion commands. If a stuck or obstructed control is found and corrected, test motion safely and document the accessory or control issue.

steps.title: Check for Control Mode or System State Issues
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is in the correct operating mode for C-arm movement. Verify that the system is not in a mode that intentionally limits positioner movement, such as a parked state, service prompt, startup interlock, active acquisition lockout, stored position conflict, or workflow step requiring operator confirmation. Expected outcome: Movement is not being blocked by an expected system state or operator workflow condition.

steps.title: Verify Brake Behavior Safely
steps.instructions: With the area clear and no patient risk present, observe whether the brake releases and holds as expected during permitted movement. Do not attempt to defeat a brake, mechanically force the C-arm, or manually push the positioner beyond normal operator-controlled movement. Expected outcome: Brake behavior is confirmed as either normal, not releasing, not holding, or intermittent. If the brake does not hold reliably, remove the system from service.

steps.title: Check Power and System Readiness
steps.instructions: Confirm the ARTIS zee system is fully powered, not in partial startup, and not showing room control, generator, stand, table, or communication faults. Verify the operator console, system control panels, and room controls are active and responsive. Expected outcome: Positioner movement is not being affected by incomplete startup or system communication failure.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart Only if Appropriate
steps.instructions: If there is no patient risk, no active procedure, no emergency condition, and site policy allows, perform a normal system restart. Allow the system to complete startup without interrupting initialization. Expected outcome: Temporary software or communication lockups clear, and the positioner becomes available again. If the fault clears after restart, perform a limited functional check, confirm no repeated fault, document the event, and return to service only if safe.

steps.title: Test Movement Through a Limited Safe Range
steps.instructions: With the room clear and clinical staff aware, test only the movement needed to confirm safe operation. Check for: Smooth C-arm motion; Proper brake release; Brake holding after movement; No unexpected drift; No repeated collision warning; No unusual noise, vibration, smell, or heat; Accurate response to controls. Expected outcome: The system either passes a safe basic motion check or repeats the fault. If movement is abnormal, stop testing and escalate.

steps.title: Do Not Continue if Motion Safety Is Questionable
steps.instructions: If the C-arm moves unpredictably, brakes intermittently, fails to stop, drifts, triggers repeated collision warnings, or loses position feedback, remove the system from clinical use. Expected outcome: A potentially unsafe motion system is not used for patient procedures.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Siemens ARTIS zee C-arm stopped during positioning and displayed a collision or positioner movement fault.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found the C-arm travel path was blocked by a mobile equipment cart near the table-side clearance area.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the obstruction, confirmed emergency stops were released, verified safe limited C-arm motion, and returned the system to service.

helpfulDetails: Exact fault message or error code displayed
Whether a patient was on the table
C-arm position when the fault occurred
Brake release and brake hold behavior
Any unusual noise, vibration, heat, or smell
Whether the fault repeated after testing

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS zee Series Angiography System - X-Ray Unavailable / Fluoro Inhibit / Exposure Abort
description: Troubleshooting ARTIS zee X-ray unavailable, fluoroscopy inhibit, or exposure abort issues caused by setup, safety interlocks, accessories, or room conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm whether a patient is on the angiography table or an active procedure is underway. If fluoroscopy or exposure is unavailable during a case, notify the physician and clinical team immediately. Do not continue relying on the system until imaging availability is restored. Expected outcome: Patient care is protected before troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Reported Symptom
steps.instructions: Ask staff what occurred and when it occurred. Determine whether the issue is: Fluoro pedal pressed but no radiation generated; Exposure button pressed but acquisition aborts; X-ray unavailable message at the console; System ready indicator not achieved; Radiation warning indicator absent; Intermittent fluoro during positioning; Abort occurs only during a specific protocol or acquisition mode. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before resets or escalation.

steps.title: Check for Active System Messages or Inhibits
steps.instructions: Review the ARTIS console, room display, and acquisition workstation for active warnings, interlock messages, or inhibit conditions. Look for messages related to: Door or room interlock; Emergency stop; Collision protection; C-arm position; Table position; Detector position; Tube cooling or heat load; Generator readiness; Footswitch or hand switch status; Patient data or exam selection. Expected outcome: Any visible inhibit condition is identified and addressed first. If the message clearly identifies a safety interlock, correct that condition and retest. If resolved, return the system to clinical use and stop.

steps.title: Verify Room Door and Radiation Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Confirm the procedure room door is fully closed and any site-specific radiation safety interlocks are satisfied. Check that warning lights, door switches, and room interlock indicators appear normal. Expected outcome: X-ray generation is not being blocked by a room safety condition. If closing the door or clearing the room interlock restores fluoroscopy, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop Buttons
steps.instructions: Inspect accessible emergency stop buttons at the table, control area, and room control locations. Confirm none are pressed, partially latched, damaged, or recently activated. Expected outcome: The system is not being inhibited by an emergency stop condition. If an emergency stop was active, reset it according to site procedure, allow the system to recover, and verify normal operation.

steps.title: Confirm System Is Fully Ready
steps.instructions: Verify the ARTIS zee system, generator, acquisition workstation, and image display chain are fully powered and ready. Check for normal startup completion, no pending initialization messages, and no system “not ready” condition. Expected outcome: The system has completed startup and is ready to generate X-rays. If the system is still initializing or not fully ready, allow startup to complete before retesting.

steps.title: Verify Correct Exam and Acquisition Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm a valid patient/exam is selected and the requested imaging mode is properly loaded. Check whether the issue occurs in all modes or only with one protocol, such as: Fluoroscopy; Digital acquisition; Roadmap; DSA; 3D acquisition; Single shot exposure. Expected outcome: The issue is not caused by an incomplete exam setup or invalid acquisition selection. If selecting a valid exam or different protocol restores imaging, document the workflow issue and stop.

steps.title: Check Footswitch and Hand Switch Operation
steps.instructions: Inspect the fluoro footswitch and exposure hand switch for physical damage, stuck buttons, fluid contamination, loose cable routing, or disconnected connectors. If an alternate known-good switch is available and approved for that room, swap it and retest. Expected outcome: The exposure command device is confirmed functional. If the alternate switch restores fluoroscopy or exposure, remove the faulty switch from use and document the accessory failure.

steps.title: Check Table, C-Arm, and Detector Positioning
steps.instructions: Confirm the C-arm, table, detector, and tube are in a valid imaging position. Look for collision protection warnings, end-of-travel limits, detector clearance issues, or table positions that may prevent exposure. Expected outcome: The system geometry allows safe X-ray generation. If repositioning the C-arm, table, or detector clears the inhibit, verify function and stop.

steps.title: Inspect Collision Protection and Obstruction Areas
steps.instructions: Visually inspect around the C-arm, detector, tube, table, floor stand, ceiling stand, cables, foot controls, and accessories. Look for drapes, equipment, lead shields, carts, trash bins, or positioning aids contacting the system. Expected outcome: No external obstruction is causing a collision inhibit or movement-related X-ray block. If removing the obstruction restores system readiness, document the cause and stop.

steps.title: Check Image Display and Acquisition Workstation Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the acquisition workstation is responsive and not frozen, locked, or displaying an unresolved software prompt. Verify images can display normally and the system is not waiting for user input, storage confirmation, patient data correction, or a DICOM/network action. Expected outcome: X-ray is not being inhibited by an unavailable acquisition or image handling workflow. If clearing the workstation prompt restores imaging, document the issue and stop.

steps.title: Check Detector and Image Receptor Readiness
steps.instructions: Confirm the detector is recognized, positioned correctly, and not reporting calibration, communication, or temperature-related faults. Expected outcome: The image receptor is available to receive images. If detector readiness cannot be confirmed, do not continue exposure attempts. Remove the system from use and escalate.

steps.title: Check Tube Heat or Cooling Messages
steps.instructions: Review the console for tube heat, cooling delay, generator, or thermal protection messages. High procedure load may temporarily inhibit exposure until the tube cools. Expected outcome: Any temporary heat-related exposure block is identified. If the system indicates tube cooling is required, allow the required cooling period and retest when the system reports ready.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Retest Only When Safe
steps.instructions: With clinical staff aware and no patient dependent on the system, perform a safe functional check using the site’s approved process. Confirm whether fluoroscopy, exposure, image display, and radiation indicators behave normally. Expected outcome: The system either returns to normal function or the fault is confirmed. If the system passes and no fault returns, document the checks performed and return to service per site policy.

steps.title: Avoid Repeated Exposure Attempts During an Unknown Fault
steps.instructions: If fluoro remains inhibited, exposure aborts repeatedly, or X-ray unavailable messages return, stop retesting. Repeated attempts may delay patient care and can mask an intermittent system, generator, detector, or safety interlock fault. Expected outcome: Troubleshooting stops before unnecessary risk or downtime confusion occurs.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Siemens ARTIS zee Series would not generate fluoroscopy and displayed an X-ray unavailable message during room setup.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found the room door interlock was not satisfied because the procedure room door was not fully latched.
CCR.Resolution: Door was secured, system readiness returned, fluoroscopy function was verified with staff, and the angiography system was returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Exact X-ray unavailable, fluoro inhibit, or exposure abort message
Whether issue occurred during setup or active procedure
Fluoro pedal and hand switch checked
Whether issue affected all modes or only one protocol

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS zee Series Angiography System - Flat detector not ready, calibration fault, or detector communication issue
description: Troubleshooting detector readiness, calibration, image receptor, or detector communication faults before escalating for angiography system repair.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not being used on an active patient before troubleshooting. If the detector fault occurs during a procedure, stop use only when clinically safe, support the clinical team, and move patient care to another room or approved backup workflow if needed. Expected outcome: No patient care depends on an angiography system with an unresolved detector or imaging fault.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Detector Fault
steps.instructions: Ask staff what message appeared and when it occurred. Check whether the issue is: Flat detector not ready; Detector not initialized; Calibration required or failed; Image receptor unavailable; Detector communication error; No image after exposure attempt; Intermittent detector readiness during positioning. Expected outcome: The fault type and timing are clearly identified before troubleshooting continues.

steps.title: Check System Startup State
steps.instructions: Verify the ARTIS zee system completed normal startup and that the acquisition workstation, generator, table, stand, and imaging chain are all online. A detector may appear unavailable if the system is still initializing or if another subsystem did not complete startup. Expected outcome: The detector fault is confirmed after the full angiography system is ready. If the detector becomes ready after full startup completes, document the delay and stop.

steps.title: Look for Active System Interlocks or Warnings
steps.instructions: Review the operator console and acquisition workstation for active inhibit, emergency stop, collision, room control, or stand/table warnings. Detector readiness may be blocked if the system is in an unsafe or incomplete operating state. Expected outcome: No unrelated system interlock is preventing detector operation. If an interlock is found and safely cleared, retest detector readiness.

steps.title: Verify Detector Position and Stand Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the C-arm, stand, and detector are in a valid clinical position and not between travel limits or caught in an incomplete movement state. Check for mechanical obstruction around the detector housing, C-arm, table, floor stand, ceiling stand, and patient area. Expected outcome: The detector is physically positioned where the system can recognize and use it safely. If repositioning clears the fault, document the cause and stop.

steps.title: Check for Collision Protection or Obstruction Activation
steps.instructions: Inspect whether collision protection, pressure-sensitive covers, detector housing contact points, or room equipment may be triggering a safety stop. Remove linen, lead aprons, arm boards, sterile covers, equipment carts, cable loops, or accessories that may interfere with detector movement or clearance. Expected outcome: Detector motion and readiness are not blocked by an external obstruction.

steps.title: Confirm Detector Covers and Accessories Are Properly Installed
steps.instructions: Inspect removable detector covers, sterile drapes, grids, table accessories, and positioning aids. Make sure nothing is loose, misaligned, pressing on the detector housing, or preventing normal detector travel. Expected outcome: Accessories do not interfere with detector recognition, cooling, movement, or image acquisition.

steps.title: Check Visible Detector Cabling and Connection Points
steps.instructions: Without opening covers or accessing internal electronics, inspect visible external cable paths for damage, strain, pinching, or loose connectors. Pay close attention to cables routed near moving stand components, table edges, cable carriers, and floor/ceiling movement areas. Expected outcome: No obvious external cable damage or disconnection is found. If a visible cable is damaged, remove the system from service and escalate for repair.

steps.title: Check Acquisition Workstation Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the acquisition workstation is responsive and not frozen, logged out, or displaying application errors. Detector communication may depend on the workstation, image acquisition software, and system control network being fully available. Expected outcome: The workstation is operating normally and communicating with the imaging system. If the workstation is frozen or partially loaded, follow approved site restart procedures only when no patient is active.

steps.title: Verify Network and Room Communication Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for room control, imaging system, DICOM, workstation, or network communication warnings. A detector communication fault may be part of a larger system communication issue rather than a detector-only failure. Expected outcome: Communication status is normal, or the broader communication issue is identified.

steps.title: Confirm Detector Temperature or Cooling Warnings
steps.instructions: Look for warnings related to detector temperature, system cooling, cabinet ventilation, or room temperature. Flat detector readiness may be affected if temperature limits are exceeded or cooling is impaired. Expected outcome: No thermal or cooling-related warning is preventing detector operation. If unusual heat, blocked vents, fan failure, or a hot electronics cabinet is noted, remove the system from service.

steps.title: Review Calibration Message Carefully
steps.instructions: If the system requests calibration, confirm whether it is routine calibration, startup calibration, detector offset/gain-related correction, or a failed calibration attempt. Do not repeatedly run calibrations without understanding whether the system is failing the same step. Expected outcome: The calibration condition is identified and not treated as a simple operator prompt if repeated failures occur.

steps.title: Check Calibration Conditions
steps.instructions: If calibration is allowed by site procedure, confirm the detector area is clear, no patient or staff are in the beam path, required covers or grids are in the correct state, and the room is safe for calibration. Expected outcome: Calibration is attempted only under safe and appropriate conditions. If calibration completes successfully and detector readiness returns, document the action and stop.

steps.title: Retest Basic Imaging Readiness Without a Patient
steps.instructions: After external checks, confirm whether the system shows detector ready status and whether fluoro or acquisition readiness returns according to site policy. Do not perform unnecessary radiation exposure. Use approved test or QA workflow only if required and safe. Expected outcome: The system either returns to ready status or the detector fault remains confirmed.

steps.title: Check for Recurrence After Movement
steps.instructions: If the detector becomes ready, gently reposition the C-arm or stand through normal clinical movements without a patient. Watch for detector communication faults that return during movement. Expected outcome: Detector communication remains stable during normal positioning. If the fault returns during movement, suspect cable chain, detector communication, encoder, stand interface, or internal detector-related failure and escalate.

steps.title: Document Error Details Before Escalation
steps.instructions: Record the exact detector message, time of occurrence, room, system state, whether the issue followed movement, whether calibration was attempted, and whether workstation or room communication warnings were present. Expected outcome: Siemens service or internal imaging support receives useful fault history instead of a vague detector complaint.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Siemens ARTIS zee flat detector was not ready and the system displayed a detector communication fault during room setup.
CCR.Cause: External inspection found no obstruction, no visible cable damage, no active collision stop, and calibration failed again under safe test conditions.
CCR.Resolution: ARTIS zee system was removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and escalated to Siemens service for detector communication and calibration fault evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Exact detector fault or calibration message
Whether issue occurred at startup, during positioning, or before exposure
Whether detector ready status returned temporarily
C-arm or stand position when the fault appeared
Any collision or emergency stop indicators
Any unusual heat, fan noise, smell, or cabinet warning

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS zee Series Angiography System - Patient table movement, lock, tilt, or tabletop positioning fault
description: Troubleshooting ARTIS zee patient table movement, locking, tilt, or tabletop positioning faults caused by setup, obstruction, controls, or safety interlocks.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm whether a patient is on the angiography table. If table motion, lock release, tilt, or tabletop positioning is unreliable during a procedure, stop table movement immediately and notify clinical staff. Expected outcome: The patient is protected from unintended movement, incomplete locking, or unsafe table positioning. If the issue affects an active procedure, do not continue troubleshooting until the patient is safe and the clinical team determines it is appropriate.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Table Fault
steps.instructions: Ask staff what table function failed and when it occurred. Identify whether the issue involves: Table will not move; Tabletop longitudinal or lateral travel is limited; Table lock will not engage or release; Tilt function is unavailable or stops unexpectedly; Table position is not recognized by the system; Movement occurs from one control but not another; Collision, brake, or safety message appears. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly defined before parts, motors, or internal controls are suspected.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions Around the Table
steps.instructions: Inspect the floor area, table base, foot pedals, accessory rails, cable drapes, sterile covers, step stools, trash bins, and equipment carts. Make sure nothing is restricting table travel, tilt clearance, or tabletop movement. Expected outcome: The table has a clear path for safe movement. If removing an obstruction restores normal motion, verify table operation with staff and stop.

steps.title: Check Patient, Accessory, and Procedure Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm that patient positioning aids, arm boards, table pads, head holders, straps, radiation shields, and sterile field accessories are not interfering with table movement or locks. For tabletop movement faults, check whether accessories are clamped too tightly or positioned against moving sections. Expected outcome: Accessories are secure but not mechanically blocking table motion. If repositioning an accessory resolves the fault, document the cause and stop.

steps.title: Verify Table Lock Status
steps.instructions: Check whether the tabletop, table base, or tilt lock is engaged when staff are attempting movement. Confirm staff are using the correct lock release or table motion control for the intended movement. Expected outcome: The table is not being commanded to move while a required lock remains engaged. If the lock status was the issue, return the system to normal use after confirming proper movement.

steps.title: Check the Table Control Devices
steps.instructions: Test table movement from the available external controls, such as the table-side controls, footswitch, control module, or room control interface, depending on site configuration. Determine whether the fault occurs from all controls or only one control. Expected outcome: A failed control source is separated from a general table movement problem. If one control device fails but another works properly, remove the faulty control device or station from use if possible and escalate that control issue for repair.

steps.title: Inspect External Control Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check visible table control cables, footswitch cables, hand control cables, and room control connections for loose plugs, damage, pinching, fluid exposure, or strain. Do not open internal covers or defeat safety circuits. Expected outcome: External communication and control connections are secure and undamaged. If reseating a loose external connector restores function, verify the repair with several safe table movements and document the finding.

steps.title: Look for Collision or Safety Interlock Messages
steps.instructions: Review the angiography system display, room control panel, and table status messages for collision protection, brake, lock, limit, tilt, or positioning warnings. Collision protection or limit messages may intentionally inhibit motion to prevent contact with the C-arm, detector, ceiling stand, floor stand, patient, or room accessories. Expected outcome: Safety-related motion inhibit conditions are identified before further movement is attempted. If a safety message clears after moving equipment to a safe position, test table motion carefully and stop.

steps.title: Check C-Arm, Detector, and Room Equipment Position
steps.instructions: Confirm the C-arm, detector, tube, monitors, shields, and ceiling-mounted equipment are not in a position that prevents table motion. Move system components only with clinical authorization and according to site procedure. Expected outcome: Table movement is not blocked by room geometry or collision protection. If repositioning the room equipment resolves the table issue, return the room to clinical staff and document the cause.

steps.title: Verify Power and System Readiness
steps.instructions: Confirm the ARTIS zee system is fully powered, initialized, and not in emergency stop, standby, service, or incomplete startup state. Check whether other system functions are also unavailable. Expected outcome: The table fault is not caused by a broader room power, startup, or system readiness problem. If the full system is not ready, address the power or startup issue first.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop and Safety Release Conditions
steps.instructions: Inspect accessible emergency stop buttons and safety stop controls in the room. Confirm none are activated. Expected outcome: Table motion is not being inhibited by an active emergency stop condition. If an emergency stop was activated, reset only after confirming the room is safe and follow site procedure before returning to clinical use.

steps.title: Check for Unusual Sounds, Heat, Smell, or Mechanical Binding
steps.instructions: During a safe test movement, listen for grinding, clicking, brake chatter, motor strain, or abnormal stopping. Check for unusual heat or odor near accessible external areas. Expected outcome: Signs of mechanical or electrical failure are identified without internal disassembly. If abnormal sound, heat, smell, or binding is present, stop testing and remove the system from service.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Functional Check
steps.instructions: With no patient on the table and the area clear, test the affected movement only as far as needed: Tabletop longitudinal travel; Tabletop lateral travel; Table height if applicable; Tilt if applicable; Lock engage and release; Position indication or system recognition. Expected outcome: The reported table function either operates normally or the fault is confirmed. If normal operation is restored, document the checks performed and return the room to clinical staff per site policy.

steps.title: Do Not Bypass Table Safety Features
steps.instructions: Do not defeat collision protection, lock sensors, emergency stops, motion limits, or interlocks to force table movement. Expected outcome: Troubleshooting remains safe and appropriate for Clinical Engineering. If a safety feature appears to be preventing use and the reason is not externally obvious, escalate for Siemens service or qualified bench/system evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Siemens ARTIS zee table would not release the tabletop lock during patient setup.
CCR.Cause: Found a procedure accessory clamp interfering with tabletop lock release; no internal service access was performed.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the obstruction, verified tabletop lock and movement operation with no patient on the table, and returned the room to service.

helpfulDetails: Patient present or no patient present during fault
Tabletop longitudinal or lateral movement result
C-arm or detector position at time of fault
Unusual sounds, heat, smell, or binding

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS zee Series Angiography System - syngo workstation, image acquisition, DICOM, or archive send failure
description: Troubleshooting workstation, acquisition, DICOM, or archive transfer issues using safe external checks before service escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm whether a patient case is active, paused, or completed. If imaging is needed for an active procedure and acquisition, review, or image transfer is unreliable, notify the clinical team immediately and follow the site’s downtime or alternate imaging workflow. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed or dependent on an unreliable imaging workstation or archive connection.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Reported Failure
steps.instructions: Ask the technologist or interventional staff what failed and when it occurred. Identify whether the issue involves: syngo workstation frozen or slow; Image acquisition not starting; Images acquired but not displaying; Prior images unavailable; DICOM send failed; PACS archive unavailable; Worklist not loading; Images stuck in queue; Network or archive error message. Expected outcome: The failure is clearly identified before troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Check Whether Imaging Acquisition Is Still Available
steps.instructions: Confirm whether fluoroscopy or acquisition can still be performed safely from the system controls. Do not assume a DICOM or PACS issue means X-ray generation has failed. Expected outcome: The issue is separated into either an acquisition problem or a post-acquisition transfer/archive problem. If imaging cannot be acquired at all, continue with acquisition checks. If images acquire normally but will not send, focus on DICOM/archive checks.

steps.title: Verify Workstation Power and Display Status
steps.instructions: Check that the syngo workstation, monitors, keyboard, mouse, and any connected review consoles are powered on. Look for blank screens, frozen images, no signal messages, failed logins, or unresponsive input devices. Expected outcome: The workstation and displays are powered and responsive. If a loose display cable, switched-off monitor, or unresponsive keyboard/mouse is corrected and normal operation returns, document and stop.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Workstation Input or Peripheral Issues
steps.instructions: Verify the keyboard, mouse, control panel, and acquisition workstation input devices are connected and functioning. Check for stuck keys, unplugged USB devices, damaged cables, or liquids near the workstation. Expected outcome: The workstation can receive normal operator input. If replacing or reconnecting an external input device restores control, document and stop.

steps.title: Review the Workstation Error Message
steps.instructions: Record the exact syngo, acquisition, DICOM, network, or archive message shown on screen. Note whether the error appears during patient registration, image acquisition, image review, send, archive, or query/retrieve. Expected outcome: The issue is documented accurately for escalation or repair.

steps.title: Check Patient and Exam Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct patient, procedure, study, and exam step are selected. Make sure the operator is not attempting to acquire images under the wrong patient, closed exam, canceled procedure, or incomplete worklist entry. Expected outcome: Acquisition and image routing are being attempted under the correct patient context. If correcting patient or exam selection resolves the issue, document and stop.

steps.title: Check Local Image Storage or Queue Status
steps.instructions: Review whether images are stored locally on the acquisition workstation but not transferred. Look for a DICOM send queue, archive queue, failed transfer list, or pending studies. Expected outcome: You can determine whether images are missing, unsent, or only delayed in transfer. If images are present locally, protect the study data and avoid unnecessary rebooting until the case status is understood.

steps.title: Verify Network Cable and Connection Status
steps.instructions: Inspect accessible network cables at the workstation, network wall jack, switch port, or equipment rack if safely available. Look for unplugged cables, damaged connectors, disconnected patch cords, or missing link lights. Expected outcome: The workstation has a physical network connection. If reconnecting a loose network cable restores DICOM send, worklist, or archive communication, document and stop.

steps.title: Check Whether Other Systems Are Affected
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether other imaging systems, PACS workstations, modality worklists, or archive sends are failing. This helps determine whether the issue is local to the ARTIS zee system or part of a broader network, PACS, RIS, or DICOM outage. Expected outcome: The issue is narrowed to either the angiography system or an external hospital network/archive problem.

steps.title: Verify Worklist Functionality
steps.instructions: Check whether the ARTIS zee system can retrieve the modality worklist. If worklist is unavailable, confirm whether manual patient entry is allowed by site policy and whether the case can proceed safely. Expected outcome: Patient registration and procedure setup are confirmed. If only worklist is down but manual entry and imaging are functioning, document the workaround and escalate the worklist/network issue.

steps.title: Check DICOM Destination Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm that the correct archive, PACS, or DICOM destination is selected. Make sure images are not being sent to a test destination, old archive, offline node, or incorrect department destination. Expected outcome: Images are routed to the intended archive destination. If selecting the correct DICOM destination resolves the issue, document and stop.

steps.title: Attempt a Controlled Resend if Appropriate
steps.instructions: If images are present locally and the patient case is not actively dependent on immediate image review, attempt a normal resend from the workstation queue if permitted by site procedure. Do not delete images or clear queues unless directed by approved service or PACS support workflow. Expected outcome: The study either transfers successfully or produces a repeatable failure message.

steps.title: Check for Archive or PACS Downtime Notices
steps.instructions: Contact PACS, imaging informatics, or IT support to confirm whether there is a known archive, network, RIS, or DICOM service outage. Expected outcome: External system outages are ruled in or ruled out before equipment repair is assumed. If PACS or network downtime is confirmed, document the device as operational with external archive issue and follow site downtime procedure.

steps.title: Confirm Image Acquisition Hardware Is Not the Primary Issue
steps.instructions: If images are not being created at all, verify that the system is not reporting detector, acquisition, exposure, or X-ray inhibit faults. A workstation or archive complaint may actually be secondary to no image acquisition. Expected outcome: The issue is correctly identified as either acquisition failure or image transfer failure.

steps.title: Perform a Safe Application Restart Only if Approved
steps.instructions: If the syngo application is frozen or unresponsive and no active case data is at risk, follow site-approved restart procedures. Coordinate with clinical staff before restarting any workstation or acquisition software. Expected outcome: The workstation returns to normal operation without interrupting patient care or losing study data. If the restart resolves the issue and images are confirmed saved or transferred, document and stop.

steps.title: Avoid Unapproved Deletions, Reconfiguration, or Deep Service Changes
steps.instructions: Do not delete studies, clear databases, modify AE titles, change IP settings, alter DICOM configuration, or perform service-level software actions unless authorized. Expected outcome: Patient data and system configuration are protected.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the ARTIS zee syngo workstation acquired images but failed to send the study to PACS after the procedure.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found the study present locally with failed DICOM queue attempts and no physical network issue at the workstation.
CCR.Resolution: Verified local image availability, documented the failed archive behavior, notified PACS/IT and Siemens service, and left the system out of service pending correction.

helpfulDetails: Whether a patient case was active
Exact syngo or DICOM error message
Whether DICOM send queue showed failed, pending, or completed status
Network cable and link light status
Whether other modalities had the same issue
Any recent network, PACS, software, or power outage

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS Q Series Angiography System - C-arm, floor/ceiling stand, or biplane positioning fault
description: Troubleshooting ARTIS Q positioning faults involving C-arm motion, stand movement, collision detection, table clearance, or biplane alignment.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the ARTIS Q Series positioning fault occurs during a procedure, stop system movement immediately. Confirm the patient, sterile field, staff, anesthesia equipment, lines, tubing, and accessories are not at risk from unintended C-arm, stand, table, or detector movement. Expected outcome: Patient and staff safety are protected before any troubleshooting continues.

steps.title: Confirm Whether the System Is Single-Plane or Biplane
steps.instructions: Identify whether the reported fault involves a single C-arm, floor-mounted stand, ceiling-mounted stand, lateral plane, frontal plane, or biplane positioning relationship. This matters because a biplane system may prevent motion if one plane is not parked, synchronized, or clear. Expected outcome: The affected plane or stand is clearly identified.

steps.title: Verify the Exact Reported Fault
steps.instructions: Ask the technologist or interventional team what occurred. Confirm whether the issue involves: C-arm will not move; Stand movement is slow, stopped, or interrupted; Positioning preset will not execute; Biplane movement is blocked; Collision warning is active; Table or detector clearance issue; Position does not match selected protocol; Joystick, control module, or foot control does not command movement. Expected outcome: The symptom is documented before resets or service escalation.

steps.title: Check for Patient, Staff, or Equipment Obstruction
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the full travel path of the C-arm, detector, X-ray tube, stand, table, monitor boom, anesthesia equipment, IV poles, lead shields, stools, cables, and floor mats. Remove only safe, non-sterile obstructions with clinical approval. Expected outcome: The system has a clear movement path. If clearing the obstruction resolves the positioning fault, return the system to clinical staff after verifying normal movement and stop.

steps.title: Check Collision Detection or Safety Interlock Messages
steps.instructions: Review the operator display for collision, clearance, stand, table, detector, or movement inhibit messages. Do not bypass, force, or repeatedly command movement against an active collision warning. Expected outcome: Any safety-related movement inhibit is recognized and respected.

steps.title: Confirm Emergency Stop Status
steps.instructions: Check all accessible emergency stop buttons, including those near the table, system stand, wall control area, and operator control location. Make sure no emergency stop is pressed or latched. Expected outcome: Emergency stop status is normal. If an emergency stop was engaged, release it only after confirming why it was pressed and that it is safe to resume movement.

steps.title: Verify Room Power and System Ready State
steps.instructions: Confirm the ARTIS Q system is powered on, fully booted, and not in a shutdown, standby, service, or faulted state. Check for abnormal indicator lights, console warnings, UPS/power events, or room power issues. Expected outcome: The system is ready to accept positioning commands.

steps.title: Check the Operator Control Module and Joystick Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the table-side control module, joystick, hand controls, and control panel for stuck buttons, fluid contamination, physical damage, or blocked motion controls. Confirm the selected control location is active and not locked out by another control station. Expected outcome: Movement commands are available from the intended control point.

steps.title: Check Footswitch and Cable Placement
steps.instructions: Verify footswitches, cables, and floor accessories are not trapped under the table base, stand base, C-arm path, or staff wheels. A cable under the stand or table can trigger mechanical resistance, movement interruption, or collision concerns. Expected outcome: No external cable or accessory is restricting motion.

steps.title: Confirm Table Position and Clearance
steps.instructions: Check table height, longitudinal/lateral position, cradle position, and accessory rails. Move the table only when clinically safe and with staff awareness. Expected outcome: The table is not blocking detector, tube, C-arm, or stand movement. If repositioning the table restores normal C-arm travel, verify safe operation and stop.

steps.title: Check Detector and Tube Clearance
steps.instructions: Inspect the detector, tube housing, covers, drapes, sterile barriers, and mounted accessories. Confirm nothing is caught between the detector and patient, table, arm board, procedure equipment, or shield. Expected outcome: Detector and tube movement are physically clear.

steps.title: Review Preset or Programmed Positioning Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm the selected position, stored working angle, park position, or biplane movement preset is appropriate for the current table and room setup. A stored position may be blocked if the table, patient, accessories, or second plane are not in the expected location. Expected outcome: The requested movement matches the actual room setup.

steps.title: For Biplane Systems, Check Both Planes
steps.instructions: Confirm the frontal and lateral planes are each recognized, clear, and not generating separate collision or positioning warnings. Verify one plane is not parked, angled, or positioned in a way that blocks the other. Expected outcome: Both planes are safe and available for coordinated movement.

steps.title: Check for Recent Accessory or Room Setup Changes
steps.instructions: Ask whether a new table accessory, arm board, injector, anesthesia setup, monitor boom position, shield, or surgical equipment was added before the fault occurred. Expected outcome: Any recent setup change that could interfere with movement is identified.

steps.title: Attempt a Controlled Basic Movement Test
steps.instructions: When no patient safety risk exists, attempt a small, slow movement using standard controls. Observe whether movement is smooth, blocked, noisy, delayed, or immediately stopped by a warning. Expected outcome: The fault is confirmed as either cleared, externally restricted, or still present. If normal motion returns and no warnings remain, document the external cause and return the room to clinical use.

steps.title: Perform a Safe System Reset Only If Appropriate
steps.instructions: If the system is not in active patient use and clinical staff agree, perform the site-approved restart or reset process for the angiography system. Do not repeatedly reset a system with active collision, motor, encoder, brake, or stand faults. Expected outcome: Temporary control or software communication faults may clear without deeper intervention.

steps.title: Verify Motion After Reset
steps.instructions: After restart, confirm the ARTIS Q reaches ready state and performs basic positioning movement without warnings. Expected outcome: The positioning system operates normally and safely. If the same fault returns, remove the system from service and escalate.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the ARTIS Q biplane system would not move the lateral C-arm into the selected working position during case setup.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found a monitor boom and floor cable positioned in the lateral plane travel path, causing a collision warning and movement inhibit.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared the obstruction with staff approval, verified basic frontal and lateral plane movement, confirmed no active positioning warnings, and returned the system to service.

helpfulDetails: Exact positioning fault or warning text
Whether the fault involved frontal plane, lateral plane, floor stand, ceiling stand, or table
Cables, shields, booms, or carts found in travel path
Any unusual sounds, vibration, jerky motion, heat, or smell

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS Q Series Angiography System - Fluoro Not Available / X-Ray Generation Or Exposure Inhibit
description: Troubleshooting fluoro unavailable, X-ray generation inhibit, exposure block, or system-not-ready conditions using safe external checks first.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm whether a patient is on the angiography table or an active procedure is underway. If fluoro or exposure becomes unavailable during a case, notify the clinical team immediately and follow the site’s emergency imaging backup process. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed by unsafe or prolonged troubleshooting during an active procedure. If the patient is unstable or the procedure is urgent, stop troubleshooting and escalate immediately.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Reported Condition
steps.instructions: Ask staff what message appeared and when the inhibit occurred. Check whether the issue involves: Fluoro pedal does not initiate imaging; X-ray exposure button does not work; System says X-ray is inhibited; Generator not ready; Tube cooling or heat warning; Door, table, C-arm, or collision-related inhibit; Acquisition available but fluoro unavailable; Fluoro available but exposure/acquisition blocked. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before power, accessory, or system readiness checks begin.

steps.title: Check Room and Radiation Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify that all procedure room doors are fully closed and that any door interlock indicators are normal. Confirm that no emergency stop, radiation disable, or room safety switch has been activated. Expected outcome: External room safety interlocks are not preventing X-ray generation. If a door or safety interlock was open and closing/resetting it restores fluoro, document and stop.

steps.title: Check the System Ready State at the Console
steps.instructions: Confirm the ARTIS Q system is fully booted and not paused at a warning, startup prompt, login screen, system message, or procedure selection screen. Review any displayed messages on the control room monitor, imaging workstation, or tableside controls. Expected outcome: The system is in a ready state and not waiting for user acknowledgement. If acknowledging a non-critical prompt restores X-ray availability, document and stop.

steps.title: Verify Footswitch and Hand Switch Operation
steps.instructions: Inspect the fluoro footswitch, exposure hand switch, and any connected control accessories. Make sure the footswitch is connected, positioned correctly, and not physically damaged, stuck, wet, or obstructed. If safe and available, try an alternate approved footswitch or hand switch. Expected outcome: A faulty or disconnected operator control is ruled out. If swapping or reseating the switch restores fluoro or exposure, document the accessory issue and remove the defective accessory from service.

steps.title: Check Tableside Control and Control Room Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm that the correct control location is active and that the system is not expecting input from a different console, tableside module, or control station. Check for active lockout, disabled control, or mode selection mismatch. Expected outcome: The intended operator control is enabled and recognized by the system.

steps.title: Check C-Arm, Table, and Collision Status
steps.instructions: Inspect the C-arm, detector, tube housing, table, and nearby equipment for possible collision or obstruction. Look for collision warnings, table position limits, parking position issues, or movement faults that may inhibit X-ray. Expected outcome: The system is not blocking exposure due to unsafe geometry, position, or collision detection. If clearing an obstruction or returning the system to a safe position restores X-ray availability, document and stop.

steps.title: Verify Required Imaging Components Are Ready
steps.instructions: Confirm that the detector, X-ray tube, collimator, generator, and image acquisition system show normal ready status. Check for obvious external indicators such as warning lights, unavailable detector status, or console messages related to image chain readiness. Expected outcome: Imaging chain readiness is confirmed before suspecting generator failure.

steps.title: Check Tube Heat, Cooling, and Dose-Related Inhibits
steps.instructions: Review the system display for tube heat, cooling delay, generator temperature, or dose-related warnings. Allow the system to cool if the message indicates a temporary thermal limit. Expected outcome: Temporary thermal protection is identified and allowed to clear without bypassing safety features. If fluoro returns after the cooldown period, document the heat or workload condition and advise staff to monitor recurrence.

steps.title: Confirm Exam, Protocol, and Mode Selection
steps.instructions: Verify that a valid exam, procedure, patient, and imaging protocol are selected. Check whether the selected mode supports fluoro or acquisition from the current system position and configuration. Expected outcome: X-ray is not inhibited by an incomplete exam setup or incompatible imaging mode.

steps.title: Check Image System and Network-Dependent Workflow Status
steps.instructions: Confirm that the imaging workstation is responsive and not frozen during patient loading, image storage, or acquisition setup. If the system is waiting for patient data, storage acknowledgement, or image system readiness, follow site downtime workflow or local reset policy. Expected outcome: X-ray generation is not blocked by a workstation or workflow readiness issue.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Reset Only If Safe
steps.instructions: If no patient is dependent on the system and site policy allows it, perform a controlled application restart or system reboot. Do not power-cycle imaging equipment during an active procedure unless directed by Siemens service or site emergency procedure. Expected outcome: Software readiness or temporary communication faults are cleared without creating patient risk. If reboot restores normal fluoro and exposure, complete operational checks, document the event, and return the system to service only if performance is stable.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Check
steps.instructions: After the issue clears, verify that fluoro, exposure control, image display, and operator controls function normally using site-approved test conditions. Confirm no active radiation safety, generator, detector, or cooling warnings remain. Expected outcome: The system is safe and ready for clinical use.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Siemens ARTIS Q system displayed an X-ray inhibit message and fluoro was not available during room setup.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found the procedure room door interlock was not satisfied because the control room door was not fully latched.
CCR.Resolution: Door was secured, interlock cleared, fluoro availability was confirmed, and the system was returned to service after a basic functional check.

helpfulDetails: Exact inhibit or generator message displayed
Whether fluoro, exposure, or both were unavailable
Whether the issue occurred during startup, room setup, or active procedure
Emergency stop or radiation disable status

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS Q Series Angiography System - Detector, dose control, or image quality calibration fault
description: Troubleshooting detector, dose control, calibration, or image quality faults using safe external checks before escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the ARTIS Q system reports a detector, dose control, or image quality calibration fault during a procedure, do not continue imaging unless the physician confirms it is clinically safe. If image quality, dose regulation, or detector response is unreliable, move patient care to an alternate imaging room or approved downtime workflow. Expected outcome: Patient care is not dependent on uncertain image quality or dose control.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Fault or Symptom
steps.instructions: Review the system message, operator report, and timing of the fault. Identify whether the issue involves: Detector not ready; Flat detector calibration failure; Dose control or automatic exposure issue; Poor image brightness or contrast; Image lag, dropout, shading, or banding; Failed image quality calibration; Error during fluoroscopy, acquisition, or startup. Expected outcome: The failure type is clearly identified before troubleshooting continues.

steps.title: Stop and Escalate Immediately for Dose Safety Concerns
steps.instructions: If the system suggests uncontrolled exposure, incorrect dose reporting, abnormal radiation output behavior, or dose regulation failure during live imaging, remove the room from clinical use. Do not attempt repeated exposures to “test” the system on a patient. Expected outcome: Radiation safety is prioritized before equipment troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check the Detector Area for Obstructions or Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the flat panel detector face, protective cover, grid area, table pad, sterile drapes, positioning accessories, and any objects between the source and detector. Remove nonessential items that could block or distort the image. Expected outcome: No external object is interfering with detector response or image calibration. If this resolves the image quality issue, document the obstruction and stop.

steps.title: Verify Proper System Positioning
steps.instructions: Confirm the C-arm, detector, table, and patient positioning are appropriate for the exam. Check for extreme angles, off-center anatomy, excessive source-to-image distance, or positioning that could affect automatic exposure control. Expected outcome: The system is positioned in a normal operating range for reliable imaging.

steps.title: Check for Collision, Table, or C-Arm Position Errors
steps.instructions: Look for collision warnings, blocked motion, incomplete positioning, or table/C-arm communication messages. Detector calibration and image quality may fail if the imaging chain is not positioned correctly. Expected outcome: The system can move and position normally without active geometry-related warnings.

steps.title: Inspect Detector and Imaging Chain Cables Externally
steps.instructions: Check visible cable routes near the detector, C-arm, table interface, and equipment cabinet area without opening covers. Look for loose connectors, pinched cables, damaged cable jackets, or cables pulled tight after room movement or cleaning. Expected outcome: No visible cable issue is affecting detector communication or image transfer.

steps.title: Confirm Room Power and System Readiness
steps.instructions: Verify the ARTIS Q system is fully powered, not partially booted, and not recovering from a power event. Check for facility power issues, tripped emergency power indicators, UPS warnings, or equipment room alarms if accessible. Expected outcome: Detector and imaging subsystems have stable power and normal startup conditions.

steps.title: Check System Warm-Up and Startup Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the system has completed normal startup, tube readiness, detector readiness, and any required warm-up prompts. Do not bypass startup messages that affect image acquisition or calibration. Expected outcome: Imaging is attempted only after the system reports ready status.

steps.title: Review Recent Cleaning, Draping, or Room Setup Changes
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether the detector cover, grid, sterile drape, table pad, or accessories were changed before the fault appeared. Also check whether a portable shield, arm board, mattress, positioning device, or lead apron was left in the imaging field. Expected outcome: Recent setup changes are ruled out as the cause.

steps.title: Check Image Quality on a Safe Test Object or Phantom
steps.instructions: When the room is clear and radiation safety procedures allow, perform a controlled image check using the site-approved test object or phantom. Do not use a patient for troubleshooting exposures. Expected outcome: The issue is confirmed under controlled conditions. If image quality is normal during the test, document the finding and investigate patient setup, positioning, or accessory-related causes.

steps.title: Compare Fluoroscopy and Acquisition Modes
steps.instructions: Determine whether the issue occurs in fluoroscopy only, acquisition only, subtraction/DSA only, roadmap only, or all imaging modes. This helps separate setup problems from detector, processing, dose control, or calibration faults. Expected outcome: The failure pattern is narrow enough to support proper escalation.

steps.title: Check Monitor and Display Chain Basics
steps.instructions: If the complaint is poor image appearance, verify the clinical monitor is powered, connected, set to the correct input, and displaying normal brightness. Confirm the issue is present on the acquisition workstation image and not only on one display. Expected outcome: A monitor/display issue is not mistaken for detector or calibration failure.

steps.title: Review Dose and Imaging Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm staff are using the expected protocol, frame rate, dose level, collimation, filtration, and magnification settings. Incorrect protocol selection can appear as poor image quality or unexpected dose behavior. Expected outcome: The selected imaging parameters match the clinical exam.

steps.title: Perform Only Approved Operator-Level Recovery
steps.instructions: If site policy allows, restart the application or system using the normal shutdown and startup process. Do not open covers, reseat internal boards, adjust calibration values, or attempt service-level detector calibration without authorization. Expected outcome: A temporary software or startup state is cleared without unsafe intervention. If the fault clears and image quality passes the site-approved check, return the system only after confirming clinical readiness.

steps.title: Document Whether the Fault Repeats
steps.instructions: If the fault returns after restart, appears during calibration, or occurs during controlled phantom imaging, remove the system from clinical service. Expected outcome: Intermittent or repeat detector, dose, or calibration faults are escalated appropriately.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Siemens ARTIS Q Series displayed a detector calibration fault and produced uneven image brightness during fluoroscopy.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found no detector obstruction, no visible cable damage, normal room power, and the fault repeated during a controlled image quality check.
CCR.Resolution: ARTIS Q Series was removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and escalated to Siemens service for detector/dose control evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Exact detector, dose, or calibration fault message
Whether the issue occurred during startup, fluoro, acquisition, DSA, or calibration
Table, C-arm, and detector position checked
Room power and startup status verified
Dose behavior or unusual exposure indications

title: Siemens Healthineers ARTIS Q Series Angiography System - Table control, collision protection, or room control communication issue
description: Troubleshooting ARTIS Q table control, collision protection, or room control communication issues using safe external checks before escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm whether a patient is on the angiography table or positioned near the C-arm, table, ceiling-mounted equipment, monitors, or room controls.

Stop all table, C-arm, and room movement if motion is unreliable, unexpected, blocked, or not responding normally.

Expected outcome: The patient and staff are protected from unintended table motion, collision risk, or incomplete system communication.

If patient safety is affected, remove the system from clinical use and escalate immediately.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Symptom
steps.instructions:
Ask the technologist, nurse, or physician what control failed and when it occurred.

Identify whether the issue involves:

Table movement not responding
Tableside control not working
C-arm or table movement inhibited
Collision protection warning
