steps.instructions: Place the detector fully into the charging bay, holder, or docking location according to normal site workflow. Confirm it is not slightly misaligned, blocked by a cover, or prevented from making contact by debris. Expected outcome: Charging begins and the system recognizes the detector charge status.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Contacts and Dock Area
steps.instructions: With the device out of clinical use, inspect the detector charging contacts and charging bay contacts. Look for: Dirt or film on contacts; Bent contacts; Broken spring contacts; Foreign objects in the dock; Damaged detector cover interfering with seating. Expected outcome: Charging surfaces are clean, aligned, and able to make contact. Do not scrape contacts aggressively or use unapproved cleaners. If contacts are damaged, escalate for repair.

steps.title: Confirm the Mobile X-Ray System Has Adequate Power
steps.instructions: Check that the MOBILETT Elara Max is powered on normally and has adequate system battery charge. If the mobile system itself is low on charge, connect it to a known-good AC outlet and allow the system to stabilize. Expected outcome: Detector charging or wireless communication is not being affected by the mobile system’s own low-power condition.

steps.title: Check the AC Outlet and Power Cord
steps.instructions: If detector charging depends on the mobile system being connected to AC, verify the cart is plugged into a working hospital-grade outlet. Check: Power cord fully seated; No visible cord damage; Outlet has power; No tripped breaker or switched outlet issue; Charging indicator on the mobile unit. Expected outcome: The mobile system is receiving AC power and can support detector charging.

steps.title: Restart the Detector Workflow From the User Interface
steps.instructions: Have the technologist exit the exam setup or detector selection screen and reselect the detector if possible. If the detector is listed but not ready, select it again or refresh the detector status using normal operator workflow. Expected outcome: A temporary detector selection or readiness state clears without further action.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Mobile System Safely
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient exam is active and no unsaved images are pending. Perform a normal shutdown and restart of the MOBILETT Elara Max. Avoid hard power interruption unless normal shutdown is not possible and local policy allows it. Expected outcome: The system software reloads and attempts to re-establish detector communication. If the detector is recognized after restart, verify readiness before returning to clinical use.

steps.title: Check Wireless Environment and Distance
steps.instructions: Keep the detector near the MOBILETT Elara Max during troubleshooting. Remove unnecessary physical barriers between the detector and mobile unit when possible. Consider whether the issue occurs only in one room, hallway, department, or area with heavy wireless traffic. Expected outcome: Detector communication improves when distance and interference are reduced.

steps.title: Check for Detector Sleep or Timeout Behavior
steps.instructions: If the detector has been idle, confirm whether it needs to be awakened by normal movement, placement, or selection from the system. Some wireless detector issues are caused by a detector being asleep, deeply discharged, or not active in the current exam workflow. Expected outcome: The detector wakes and becomes available after proper selection or charging.

steps.title: Compare With Another Known-Good Detector If Available
steps.instructions: If site workflow allows, test the MOBILETT Elara Max with another compatible known-good detector. Also test the suspect detector on its normal charging location or approved workflow. Expected outcome: If another detector works, the original detector, battery, charging contacts, or pairing may be the issue. If no detector works, the mobile system, wireless interface, charging bay, or software may be the issue.

steps.title: Check for Recent Changes
steps.instructions: Ask whether anything changed before the issue started. Examples include: Detector swapped between systems; Mobile system recently moved departments; Wireless network changes; Software update; Detector dropped; Battery recently replaced; Charging bay damaged; Issue started after cleaning. Expected outcome: Recent changes help identify whether the issue is environmental, accessory-related, or system-related.

steps.title: Verify the Error Message or Status Code
steps.instructions: Record the exact detector message shown on the system. Examples may include detector not available, detector not ready, wireless connection lost, battery low, charging fault, or detector communication failure. Expected outcome: The work order contains specific fault information instead of a vague “not working” description.

steps.title: Perform a Limited Functional Check
steps.instructions: Once the detector is recognized and charged, perform a non-patient readiness check according to site policy. Confirm: Detector is detected; Battery status is acceptable; Wireless status is stable; Detector shows ready; No active detector error remains; System allows normal exam setup. Expected outcome: The detector and mobile system are ready for clinical use. If the issue returns during readiness checks, remove the device or detector from service.

CCR.Complaint: Technologist reported the MOBILETT Elara Max wireless detector was not detected and would not become ready for portable chest exams.
CCR.Cause: Detector battery was fully depleted and the detector was not fully seated in the charging bay, preventing charging contact.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated detector in charger, verified charging indication, allowed sufficient charge time, restarted system, and confirmed detector was recognized and ready for use.

helpfulDetails: Detector serial number or asset tag
Whether the issue affects one detector or multiple detectors
Whether the detector was fully seated in the charger
Whether the detector was recently dropped or cleaned
Room or department where issue occurred
Whether AC power was connected to the mobile system

title: Siemens Healthineers MOBILETT Elara Max Mobile X-Ray System - Exposure Inhibit, Generator Fault, or Failed X-Ray Exposure
description: Troubleshooting exposure inhibit, failed X-ray exposure, generator fault, readiness, and external setup issues before repair escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the MOBILETT Elara Max is not actively positioned over a patient before troubleshooting exposure faults. If the exposure failed during a patient exam, have clinical staff stop the exam, protect the patient, and move to another available X-ray system if imaging is urgent. Expected outcome: No patient remains dependent on a mobile X-ray system that cannot safely complete an exposure.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Exposure Complaint
steps.instructions: Ask the technologist what happened and when. Determine whether the issue involves: Exposure inhibit before attempting exposure; Rotor or prep failure; X-ray exposure button not responding; Exposure started but aborted; Generator fault message; Tube warmup or tube readiness issue; Detector not ready preventing exposure; Battery or charging state preventing exposure; Intermittent failure on certain techniques only. Expected outcome: The issue is narrowed to readiness, exposure control, detector status, power state, or generator-related behavior.

steps.title: Check Whether the System Is Safe and Ready for Use
steps.instructions: Verify the unit is powered on normally and not in a startup, shutdown, charging-only, locked, service, transport, or error state. Confirm the system shows ready status before attempting any exposure-related testing. Expected outcome: The unit is in a normal operating state and not inhibited by a basic status condition.

steps.title: Review the Displayed Error or Inhibit Message
steps.instructions: Read the exact message shown on the workstation or system display. Record whether the message references generator, exposure inhibit, detector, battery, emergency stop, tube, collimator, communication, or system readiness. Expected outcome: The fault is identified by the system message instead of guessing at the cause.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop and Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Confirm any emergency stop, drive lock, brake, or safety control is not engaged. If an emergency stop or safety lock was activated, reset it according to normal operator workflow and restart the system if required by site procedure. Expected outcome: Exposure is not blocked by an active safety interlock.

steps.title: Verify Battery and AC Power Status
steps.instructions: Check battery charge level, charging indicator, and AC connection if the unit is plugged in. A low battery or unstable power condition may prevent exposure even if the workstation appears powered. Expected outcome: The system has sufficient power available for X-ray generation.

steps.title: Confirm the Detector Is Ready
steps.instructions: Verify the correct detector is selected, connected, charged, and ready. If the detector is wireless, confirm it is awake, associated with the system, and not showing a communication or battery fault. Expected outcome: Exposure is not inhibited because the detector is offline or unavailable.

steps.title: Check Exam and Technique Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the exam is properly selected and patient/exam information is entered as required by the department workflow. Verify the selected technique is appropriate and not outside normal limits. Expected outcome: The system has a valid exam and exposure setup.

steps.title: Check Collimator and Positioning Readiness
steps.instructions: Confirm the tube head, collimator, and detector are positioned normally for the intended exposure. Verify the collimator light, field alignment, and SID setup appear reasonable. Expected outcome: Exposure is not blocked by setup, positioning, or readiness conditions.

steps.title: Inspect the Exposure Switch or Hand Switch
steps.instructions: Check that the exposure switch is connected, seated, and not physically damaged. Inspect the cord for cuts, strain, crushed sections, or loose connection points. If available and approved by site practice, test with a known-good compatible exposure switch. Expected outcome: The exposure command is being sent reliably to the system.

steps.title: Check for a Stuck or Damaged Exposure Button
steps.instructions: Inspect the prep and expose positions of the exposure switch. Make sure the button is not sticking, binding, cracked, or failing to return normally. Expected outcome: The exposure control is not mechanically preventing prep or exposure.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System Only When Safe
steps.instructions: If no patient is active and no image acquisition is in progress, restart the MOBILETT Elara Max using normal shutdown and startup workflow. Do not repeatedly cycle power if the same generator fault returns. Expected outcome: A temporary software or readiness state clears, or the fault repeats and confirms a persistent issue.

steps.title: Attempt a Controlled Functional Check
steps.instructions: If permitted by site policy and radiation safety procedure, have qualified imaging staff perform a controlled test exposure using proper shielding, area control, and approved test setup. Clinical Engineering should not create unnecessary radiation exposure or bypass normal safety workflow. Expected outcome: The system either completes exposure normally or the fault is reproduced under controlled conditions.

steps.title: Look for Repeated Fault Patterns
steps.instructions: Determine whether the issue occurs: On every exposure; Only after startup; Only on battery power; Only on AC power; Only with one detector; Only at higher technique settings; Only when using the hand switch; Only after system movement. Expected outcome: The problem is narrowed to power delivery, detector readiness, exposure control, generator load, or intermittent connection behavior.

steps.title: Check for External Damage or Environmental Concerns
steps.instructions: Inspect the mobile unit for signs of impact, loose panels, damaged cables, liquid intrusion, unusual odor, excessive heat, or abnormal sounds. If burning smell, arcing sound, smoke, or unusual heat is present, stop troubleshooting immediately. Expected outcome: Obvious unsafe conditions are identified before further operation.

steps.title: Stop if Generator or Exposure Fault Returns
steps.instructions: If the MOBILETT Elara Max continues to show generator fault, exposure inhibit, failed prep, failed exposure, or exposure abort after external checks, remove the unit from service. Do not attempt internal generator, high-voltage, tube, inverter, or board-level troubleshooting in the clinical area. Expected outcome: The device is escalated before unsafe or unreliable imaging use continues.

CCR.Complaint: Technologist reported the Siemens MOBILETT Elara Max displayed an exposure inhibit message and would not complete a portable chest X-ray exposure.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found the detector ready and battery charged, but the exposure fault returned after checking setup, hand switch connection, and system restart.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the MOBILETT Elara Max from service, labeled it Out of Service, documented repeated exposure failure, and escalated the unit for generator/exposure system evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Exact exposure inhibit or generator fault message
Whether exposure failed during prep or during expose
Battery charge level at time of failure
AC power connected or battery-only operation
Detector selected, connected, and ready status
Whether a known-good exposure switch was tested
Whether fault occurs with one detector or all detectors
Any abnormal sound, odor, heat, or visible damage
Whether system restart changed the symptom

title: Siemens Healthineers MOBILETT Elara Max Mobile X-Ray System - Battery Runtime, Charging, or Power-On Failure
description: Troubleshooting power-on failure, poor battery runtime, charging issues, AC power problems, or mobile system power complaints before repair escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the MOBILETT Elara Max is not needed for an active patient exam. If the unit loses power, shuts down unexpectedly, or cannot complete imaging, have clinical staff use another mobile X-ray system if imaging is urgent. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed by a system with unreliable power.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Power Complaint
steps.instructions: Ask the operator what happened and when. Determine whether the issue involves: Unit will not power on; Unit powers on only when plugged in; Battery runtime is short; Battery will not charge; Charging indicator does not appear; System shuts down during transport or exposure preparation; Unit powers on but displays a low-battery warning; Unit was left unplugged or unused for an extended period. Expected outcome: The issue is separated into power-on, battery runtime, charging, or AC input behavior.

steps.title: Check Whether the System Is Safely Parked and Stable
steps.instructions: Make sure the unit is parked in a safe location with the brake engaged. Do not troubleshoot power or charging while the unit is blocking patient care areas, doorways, hallways, or emergency access. Expected outcome: The unit can be evaluated without creating a transport or safety hazard.

steps.title: Verify the AC Outlet
steps.instructions: Plug the MOBILETT Elara Max into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Test the outlet with another approved device or outlet tester if appropriate for your facility workflow. Expected outcome: The system receives reliable facility power. If the outlet is dead, have Facilities correct the outlet issue and stop troubleshooting the mobile X-ray system.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and Plug
steps.instructions: Check the AC power cord for: Loose connection; Damaged insulation; Bent or damaged plug blades; Evidence of overheating; Strain relief damage; Cord not fully seated at the device or wall outlet. Expected outcome: The cord is safe and properly connected. If the cord is damaged, remove it from use and replace or escalate according to site policy.

steps.title: Confirm Charging Indication
steps.instructions: With the system plugged into AC power, look for the expected charging indication on the system display or power/charging indicator. Allow the unit to remain connected long enough to determine whether charging starts. Expected outcome: The system recognizes AC power and indicates charging. If there is no charging indication on a known-good outlet, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check for Power-On Response
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on the system using the normal power button sequence. Observe whether the system: Shows no response; Displays lights only; Starts booting then shuts down; Boots normally while plugged in; Shows a battery or charging warning. Expected outcome: The power-on behavior is documented clearly for escalation if needed.

steps.title: Allow Time for a Deeply Discharged Battery
steps.instructions: If the unit was stored unplugged or left unused, leave it connected to AC power and monitor for charging response. A deeply discharged battery may not immediately provide normal runtime. Expected outcome: The system begins charging and eventually powers on. If charging resumes and runtime returns to normal, document the issue and stop.

steps.title: Check for User-Accessible Battery or Power Status Information
steps.instructions: Review the system status screen, battery icon, charging message, or operator-visible alerts. Look for warnings such as low battery, charging fault, battery service required, or power supply-related messages. Expected outcome: The displayed status helps separate normal low charge from a battery, charger, or internal power fault.

steps.title: Confirm the Unit Was Stored Correctly
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether the system is routinely returned to its charging location after use. Mobile X-ray systems can develop runtime complaints when they are left unplugged between exams or not connected fully to AC power. Expected outcome: A workflow issue is ruled in or out before assuming equipment failure.

steps.title: Check for Excessive Runtime Expectations
steps.instructions: Ask how long the system is expected to operate between charges and whether the complaint occurs after normal use, heavy use, or repeated imaging. Expected outcome: The complaint is compared against actual usage. If the device charges normally and runtime is acceptable after full charge, the issue may be user workflow rather than equipment failure.

steps.title: Inspect for Heat, Odor, or Abnormal Sounds
steps.instructions: While plugged in and during power-on attempts, check for: Burning smell; Unusual heat near the power entry area; Clicking, buzzing, or repeated relay sounds; Fan noise without boot; Intermittent shutdown. Expected outcome: Any safety concern is identified early. If heat, odor, smoke, or electrical noise is present, unplug the unit if safe, remove it from service, and escalate immediately.

steps.title: Verify Accessories Are Not Causing a Startup Delay
steps.instructions: Check whether external accessories, detector charging components, USB devices, or network peripherals were recently connected. Remove nonessential external accessories if allowed by site policy and attempt normal startup again. Expected outcome: A peripheral-related startup issue is ruled out before deeper repair evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Error Messages During Boot
steps.instructions: If the system begins to boot, document any power, battery, charger, generator, detector, or system startup messages shown on the display. Expected outcome: The exact message is captured for repair or vendor support.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Restart if the System Is Frozen
steps.instructions: If the unit appears powered but unresponsive, follow the site-approved normal shutdown or restart process. Avoid repeated hard power cycling unless required for safety or directed by approved service guidance. Expected outcome: A software lockup is ruled out. If normal function returns and charging is confirmed, document and stop.

steps.title: Verify Function After Charging
steps.instructions: If the unit begins charging normally, allow it to charge sufficiently, then confirm: System powers on; Battery indicator behaves normally; Unit remains on without immediate shutdown; No power or charging warnings remain; System can be safely returned to clinical use if all required checks pass. Expected outcome: The power issue is resolved and the device status is clear.

CCR.Complaint: Radiology reported the Siemens MOBILETT Elara Max would not hold a charge and powered down during mobile imaging rounds.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering verified the unit was connected to a known-good outlet but showed poor battery runtime after charging and displayed a battery-related warning.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the unit from service, labeled it Out of Service, documented charging behavior and warning message, and sent the system for battery and power subsystem evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Whether the AC cord was inspected or swapped
Battery percentage or displayed battery status
Whether the unit powers on while plugged in
Whether the unit powers on while unplugged
Any boot or power-related error messages
Whether the issue occurs after full charge or only after extended use
Unusual heat, odor, clicking, buzzing, or fan behavior
Whether the system was stored unplugged

title: Siemens Healthineers MOBILETT Elara Max Mobile X-Ray System - Worklist, DICOM Send, Wireless Network, or Image Export Failure
description: Troubleshooting mobile X-ray worklist, DICOM send, wireless network, archive, and image export failures before repair escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the MOBILETT Elara Max is not actively imaging a patient before restarting, changing network settings, disconnecting cables, or attempting repeated sends. If imaging was completed but images did not transmit, verify with clinical staff that the images remain available on the system before any reboot or shutdown. Expected outcome: Patient imaging data is protected before troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Confirm the Exact Communication Failure
steps.instructions: Ask the technologist what failed and when it occurred. Determine whether the issue involves: Worklist not loading; Patient demographics not importing; DICOM send failure; Images stuck in send queue; PACS destination unavailable; Wireless network not connected; Export to USB or external media failure; One study failing or all studies failing; One destination failing or all destinations failing. Expected outcome: The issue is separated into worklist, DICOM transmission, wireless network, archive destination, or local export failure.

steps.title: Check Whether the System Is in the Correct Network Area
steps.instructions: Confirm the mobile X-ray system is in an area with approved wireless coverage. Move the unit closer to a known working clinical wireless area if signal strength is weak or unavailable. Mobile imaging systems can lose connection when parked in elevators, storage rooms, older shielded areas, basement spaces, or remote hallways. Expected outcome: The system shows a stable wireless connection in an area known to support mobile imaging traffic.

steps.title: Verify Wireless Status on the System
steps.instructions: Check the system network indicator or communication status screen for wireless connection state. Confirm whether the unit is connected, disconnected, searching, or showing authentication failure. Expected outcome: The system is either confirmed connected to the hospital wireless network or clearly showing a wireless connection problem.

steps.title: Confirm the Correct Wireless Network Is Selected
steps.instructions: Verify the system is attempting to connect to the correct approved hospital SSID for medical devices. Do not connect the unit to guest Wi-Fi, public networks, or personal hotspots unless directed by IT and hospital policy. Expected outcome: The system is associated with the correct clinical network.

steps.title: Check for Obvious Wireless or Network Changes
steps.instructions: Ask whether the problem began after: Network maintenance; Access point replacement; Password or certificate change; IP addressing change; PACS or RIS downtime; System relocation to a new department; Recent service or software change. Expected outcome: Recent network or system changes are identified before assuming equipment failure.

steps.title: Verify Other Mobile Imaging Devices
steps.instructions: Ask whether other mobile X-ray units or imaging devices are having the same worklist or DICOM issue. If multiple devices are affected, contact IT, PACS, or the imaging informatics team. Expected outcome: A site-wide network, RIS, PACS, or DICOM destination issue is separated from a single-device problem.

steps.title: Check Worklist Function Separately
steps.instructions: Attempt to refresh or query the worklist using normal operator workflow. Confirm whether the system receives any patient list, no list, delayed results, or an error message. If worklist fails but image send works, suspect RIS/worklist configuration, worklist server availability, or network routing rather than full communication failure. Expected outcome: Worklist behavior is isolated from DICOM image send behavior.

steps.title: Check DICOM Send Separately
steps.instructions: Select a completed test or non-patient image only if allowed by site policy, or use an existing completed study under clinical direction. Attempt to send to the normal PACS destination. Confirm whether the send completes, stays queued, retries, fails immediately, or fails after timeout. Expected outcome: DICOM send behavior is documented clearly.

steps.title: Review the Send Queue or Transfer Status
steps.instructions: Check whether studies are waiting in the queue. Look for failed, pending, retrying, or completed status. If many studies are queued, avoid clearing or deleting them unless directed by imaging leadership and site policy. Expected outcome: Stored images are protected and the transfer failure pattern is identified.

steps.title: Confirm PACS or Archive Destination Availability
steps.instructions: Contact PACS, imaging informatics, or IT to confirm the DICOM destination is online and accepting studies. If available, compare with another imaging device sending successfully to the same destination. Expected outcome: The archive destination is confirmed available before device-side repair is considered.

steps.title: Check Date, Time, and Patient Demographics
steps.instructions: Confirm the MOBILETT Elara Max date and time are correct. Incorrect date, time, or patient demographics can affect worklist matching, study association, and archive workflow. Expected outcome: Basic system identity and timing information do not appear to be contributing to the communication failure.

steps.title: Check for Local Export Issues
steps.instructions: If the problem is USB or local image export, verify the external media is approved, formatted appropriately, and recognized by the system. Try a known-good hospital-approved USB device if permitted by site policy. Do not use personal USB drives. Expected outcome: The issue is narrowed to the export media, system USB recognition, file export workflow, or internal storage/export function.

steps.title: Inspect External Ports and Connections
steps.instructions: If wired service network or USB export is involved, inspect visible ports for damage, debris, looseness, bent contacts, or broken connectors. Do not force cables or USB devices into damaged ports. Expected outcome: Physical connection problems are identified without internal disassembly.

steps.title: Power Cycle Only When Safe
steps.instructions: If no patient exam is active and images are confirmed stored, perform a controlled shutdown and restart according to site workflow. After restart, recheck wireless connection, worklist query, DICOM send, and export behavior. Expected outcome: Temporary software or communication lockups clear after a safe restart.

steps.title: Document Any Error Codes or Messages
steps.instructions: Record the exact message shown on the MOBILETT Elara Max, including destination name, time of failure, study count in queue, wireless status, and whether worklist or DICOM send failed. Photos of the error screen may be helpful if allowed by site policy and no patient information is exposed. Expected outcome: Repair escalation includes useful details instead of a vague communication complaint.

steps.title: Do Not Change Network Configuration Without Approval
steps.instructions: Do not alter IP address, AE title, port, DICOM destination, wireless certificate, gateway, DNS, or security settings unless directed by the responsible imaging IT or service team. Expected outcome: Troubleshooting does not create additional configuration problems.

steps.title: Return to Service Only After Successful Verification
steps.instructions: If the issue is resolved, confirm with clinical staff that worklist loads, a test or approved study sends successfully, and any queued studies are handled appropriately. Expected outcome: The device is returned to service only after communication workflow is verified.

CCR.Complaint: Technologist reported the MOBILETT Elara Max would not load worklist and completed exams were not sending to PACS.
CCR.Cause: Wireless connection was weak in the storage hallway, and DICOM studies remained queued until the unit was moved to an area with confirmed hospital wireless coverage.
CCR.Resolution: Moved the unit to a known wireless coverage area, verified worklist refresh, successfully sent queued images to PACS, and returned the system to service.
